comment_text.sn3.5:1.4: For “protect themselves” (rakkhito attā) compare rakkhitatta at SN 6.13:2.4, Thag 2.11:2.4. The use of the term attā here is conventional, not metaphysical. “Personal safety” ( ātmarakṣā) is a paramount concern of kings (eg. Arthaśāstra 1.1.3, 1.21.30, 5.4.16, 7.3.24, 9.6.40, 12.2.10).

root_text.sn3.5:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho rājā pasenadi kosalo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn3.5:1.3: “idha mayhaṁ, bhante, rahogatassa paṭisallīnassa evaṁ cetaso parivitakko udapādi:
root_text.sn3.5:1.4: ‘kesaṁ nu kho rakkhito attā, kesaṁ arakkhito attā’ti?
root_text.sn3.5:1.6: ‘ye kho keci kāyena duccaritaṁ caranti, vācāya duccaritaṁ caranti, manasā duccaritaṁ caranti;
root_text.sn3.5:1.8: Kiñcāpi te hatthikāyo vā rakkheyya, assakāyo vā rakkheyya, rathakāyo vā rakkheyya, pattikāyo vā rakkheyya;
root_text.sn3.5:1.11: Bāhirā hesā rakkhā, nesā rakkhā ajjhattikā;
root_text.sn3.5:1.13: Ye ca kho keci kāyena sucaritaṁ caranti, vācāya sucaritaṁ caranti, manasā sucaritaṁ caranti;
root_text.sn3.5:1.15: Kiñcāpi te neva hatthikāyo rakkheyya, na assakāyo rakkheyya, na rathakāyo rakkheyya, na pattikāyo rakkheyya;
root_text.sn3.5:1.18: Ajjhattikā hesā rakkhā, nesā rakkhā bāhirā;
root_text.sn3.5:2.2: Ye hi keci, mahārāja, kāyena duccaritaṁ caranti …pe…
root_text.sn3.5:2.5: Bāhirā hesā, mahārāja, rakkhā, nesā rakkhā ajjhattikā;
root_text.sn3.5:2.7: Ye hi keci, mahārāja, kāyena sucaritaṁ caranti, vācāya sucaritaṁ caranti, manasā sucaritaṁ caranti;
root_text.sn3.5:2.9: Kiñcāpi te neva hatthikāyo rakkheyya, na assakāyo rakkheyya, na rathakāyo rakkheyya, na pattikāyo rakkheyya;
root_text.sn3.5:2.12: Ajjhattikā hesā, mahārāja, rakkhā, nesā rakkhā bāhirā;

translation_text.sn3.5:1.2: Seated to one side, King Pasenadi said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn3.5:1.3: “Just now, sir, as I was in private retreat this thought came to mind.
translation_text.sn3.5:1.4: ‘Who are those who protect themselves? And who are those who don’t protect themselves?’
translation_text.sn3.5:1.6: ‘Those who do bad things by way of body, speech, and mind
translation_text.sn3.5:1.8: Even if they’re protected by a company of elephants, cavalry, chariots, or infantry,
translation_text.sn3.5:1.11: Because such protection is exterior, not interior.
translation_text.sn3.5:1.12: That’s why they don’t protect themselves.
translation_text.sn3.5:1.13: Those who do good things by way of body, speech, and mind
translation_text.sn3.5:1.15: Even if they’re not protected by a company of elephants, cavalry, chariots, or infantry,
translation_text.sn3.5:1.18: Because such protection is interior, not exterior.
translation_text.sn3.5:1.19: That’s why they do protect themselves.’”
translation_text.sn3.5:2.1: “That’s so true, great king! That’s so true!” said the Buddha. And he repeated the king’s statement, adding:

reference_text.sn3.5:1.1: bj13.138, cck15.100, csp1ed12.73, csp2ed12.73, dr15.100, ms12S1_602, msdiv116, ndp12.72, pts-vp-pli1ed1.73, pts-vp-pli2ed1.167, sya15.105
